title: Chassalia princei
taxonomy: Chassalia princei is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Chassalia princei is further classified under the genus Chassalia. The genus Chassalia is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa and Southeast Asia. Chassalia princei's classification within the Rubiaceae family highlights its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this family.

intro: Chassalia princei, also known as Prince's Chassalia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Chassalia princei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Chassalia princei is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used to make capsules and tablets, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
